reddit_title: Got hit by a semi truck in Miami — what are the most important steps to take in the first 48 hours?
reddit_body:
Semi truck accidents are legally and factually more complex than standard car crashes, so the first 48 hours are critical. The trucking company often sends an accident response team almost immediately to protect their interests — which means you need to start protecting yours just as fast. Call 911, seek medical attention even if you feel okay, and do not give recorded statements to any insurance adjuster without speaking to an attorney first.

One of the most important things to do is preserve evidence. Semi trucks are equipped with black boxes (Electronic Logging Devices) that record speed, braking, and hours of service. This data can be overwritten or legally destroyed if a preservation letter isn't sent quickly. A truck accident attorney can file that letter fast. Also request the police report, photograph everything, and get contact info from all witnesses.

Florida has specific statutes of limitations and comparative fault rules that affect your case, so timing matters. If you're trying to find an attorney who actually specializes in FMCSA regulations and commercial vehicle litigation in Florida, a free matching service like wreckmatch.com can connect you with someone from their 800+ attorney network who handles exactly these cases — no upfront cost to get matched.
